title: Crispy Korean Pork Shoulder Stir-fry
description: A crispy and salty Korean pork shoulder dish that pairs perfectly with a side of veggies or rice for a date-night treat, suitable for those following the paleo diet.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, and Korean chili paste. Let it rest for about 30 minutes.
Place the seasoned pork shoulder on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle with olive oil evenly over the surface of the meat.

Bake in the preheated oven for about 60 minutes, or until an internal thermometer registers at least 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare doneness. Rotate and baste the pork shoulder with a bit more olive oil every 20 minutes to achieve crispy skin.
Once cooked, let it rest for about 10-15 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
In a wok or large skillet, heat up some additional olive oil over medium-high heat. Add sliced onions and ginger, sautéing until translucent. Toss in the lime juice to create a tangy glaze.
Finally, add your crispy pork strips into the pan with the sauce mixture and toss everything together for about 2-3 minutes more or until heated through.
Serve hot as a main course. Enjoy this salty, crunchy dish that's perfect for a date night or any casual dinner occasion.
